headline: Applied Materials Deepens AI Chip Packaging Push As Valuation Stretches
summary: Applied Materials (NasdaqGS:AMAT) has completed its acquisition of ASMPT NEXX, expanding its reach in advanced semiconductor packaging for AI and high-bandwidth chips. The company is also deepening its collaboration with SCREEN Semiconductor at the EPIC Center, aiming to tighten its role in next generation chip manufacturing flows. These moves mark a shift toward broader participation across advanced packaging and AI focused chip supply chains, beyond prior discussions centered on analyst...

headline: Memory Chip Prices Will Make or Break DRAM ETF Before Year-End 2026
summary: The Roundhill Memory ETF (CBOE:DRAM) is the first U.S.-listed pure-play memory-chip ETF, and it landed in the middle of one of the most violent upcycles the industry has ever seen. DRAM launched on April 2, 2026 with a 0.65% expense ratio, just as Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) was approaching a trillion-dollar market cap. Micron is up ... headline: Applied Materials (AMAT) Continues Its Bull Run as Analyst Sees More Upside
summary: Goldman Sachs owns around 0.31% of Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMAT) out of its portfolio, with a total portfolio value of around $2.68 billion. AMAT ranks among the top Goldman Sachs tech stocks to buy now. Applied Materials seems to be proactive amid the robust AI demand. The company expects its semiconductor equipment business to soar […]
